dailyJudgementSnippet: OpenAI wins the day: Codex got two separate front-page threads with generally positive, capability-focused discussion, while Anthropic took major heat over Claude Opus 4.7 and Gemini’s Mac app launch drew mostly complaints [E2][E3][E1][E4].
winnerExplanation: OpenAI had the strongest positive signal because Codex appeared in two meaningful front-page discussions, both with more excitement than backlash. Anthropic had more total coverage, but the dominant thread of the day was negative reaction to Claude Opus 4.7’s confusing changes, stricter refusals, and trust issues, which outweighed scattered praise for Claude Code workflows. Google Gemini’s main story was clearly negative. Microsoft Copilot had no relevant story/comment signal.

openai.judgementSnippet: Net positive: Codex drew real enthusiasm across two front-page stories, with praise for product direction and practical capability, though some commenters said it was still catching up rather than clearly leading [E2][E3].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Mixed but net negative: there were several positive Claude Code/Claude workflow anecdotes, but they were outweighed by strong backlash to Claude Opus 4.7 over confusing adaptive thinking, stricter cyber refusals, and trust/communication concerns [E1][E5][E6][E7][E8][E9][E10][E11].

google_gemini.judgementSnippet: Negative: the Gemini Mac app thread was dominated by complaints about login/data-sharing requirements, sluggishness, verification friction, and limited controls, with an extra smaller complaint about Gemini being poor at iterative slide edits [E4][E12].

evidence.summary: Claude Opus 4.7 launch thread: comments complain adaptive thinking is confusing, cybersecurity filters block legitimate research, and Anthropic has damaged trust with poor communication and unclear caps.
Codex for almost everything: commenters praise the permissions flow and background browser testing and argue this kind of agent product may take off, despite some skepticism.
Codex hacked a Samsung TV: commenters report good Codex-assisted hacking experiences and treat the result as impressive, while noting source-code access was an important advantage.

The Gemini app is now on Mac: top comments criticize required Google login/data sharing, sluggish app behavior, 18+ verification via government ID or credit card for CLI use, and limited feature/model control.
Qwen pelican comparison versus Claude Opus 4.7 is mixed: the title favors Qwen, but commenters argue Opus is more physically coherent and vastly better on coding benchmarks.
AI cybersecurity is not proof of work: commenters question the value of Anthropic’s closed Mythos/Claude framing and suggest the company benefits from hype while revealing little about setup.
Cloudflare Email Service: a commenter says emailing Claude works super well for coding, bookkeeping, and troubleshooting workflows.
Launch HN Kampala: a commenter says Claude Code analyzed HAR files, generated OpenAPI, and helped build a reliable MCP server quickly and effectively.
CodeBurn for Claude Code token usage: comments mix concern over very high Claude Code spending with a counterclaim that the $200 plan rarely hits rate limits.
Codex for almost everything comments include a direct comparison claiming Claude Desktop/Cowork already offers most of these features.
Codex hacked a Samsung TV comments include a positive Claude Code anecdote about discovering and controlling insecure Bluetooth lights.
Qwen pelican thread includes a complaint that Gemini is poor at iterative diagram edits in slides, described as a gap between toy and tool.
